workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just came out of competitive positioning with Humongous Insurance. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 15:22 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with competitive positioning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 08:33 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 11:29 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-E trap (Opportunity hypothesis from a non-commercial inquiry); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-E failure mode (Opportunity hypothesis from a non-commercial inquiry)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-E -- Opportunity hypothesis from a non-commercial inquiry
